title: Enemy
excerpt: An enemy is a creature the current game state treats as opposed to another creature for conflict, hostile targeting, or adverse effects.
markdown:
# Enemy

An enemy is a creature the current game state treats as opposed to another creature for conflict resolution. Enemy status matters when a spell, feature, or rule refers to hostile targets, damaging enemies, excluding enemies from benefits, or applying effects only to creatures on the opposing side.

## Relationship State

Enemy status is determined by the active relationship state, not by creature type. A creature can count as an enemy because of faction opposition, combat-side alignment, a control conflict, or another world-state relationship that places the creature on the opposing side for the current effect.

## Rules Use

### Hostile Targeting
- some effects can target only an enemy or hostile creature
- some effects exclude enemies from benefits or protection
- some effects distinguish enemies from allies when resolving an area or aura

### State Can Change
- enemy status can change if a creature changes sides, is controlled, or otherwise stops being treated as an opposing creature in the current situation
- a rule that depends on an enemy should use the relationship state that exists when the effect resolves

### Separate From Attitude
- an enemy often has a hostile attitude, but the two concepts are not identical
- enemy status is usually used for tactical and effect-resolution purposes, while attitude is broader and more social
